title: Redis Cache Cluster Complete Failure in Production
symptoms: Redis CLUSTERDOWN errors in all application logs
All Redis nodes reporting OOM (Out of Memory) conditions
Application response times spiking from 200ms to 8000ms
Database connection pool exhaustion due to cache fallthrough
Customer complaints about slow page loads and timeouts
root_cause: Missing TTL configuration on high-frequency session metadata cache keys causing unbounded memory growth across all Redis cluster nodes
resolution: Performed rolling restart of Redis cluster with memory limits enforced, deployed hotfix to add TTL on the affected cache key pattern, and scaled up database read replicas temporarily
preventive_actions: Enforce mandatory TTL policy on all Redis write paths via code review checklist
Implement Redis memory usage alerting at 70% and 85% thresholds
Add automated cache eviction policies as safety net
Create runbook for Redis cluster recovery procedures
Deploy Redis memory profiling in staging environments

timeline.event: Redis cluster enters CLUSTERDOWN state, all nodes OOM
Automated alerts fire for cache failure and response time degradation
On-call engineer Sarah Chen begins investigation
Root cause identified as missing TTL on session metadata keys
Database read replicas scaled up to handle cache fallthrough
Rolling restart of Redis cluster initiated with memory limits
Hotfix deployed to add TTL on affected cache key pattern
Redis cluster fully operational, response times normalizing

embedding_summary: Complete Redis cache cluster failure caused by missing TTL on session metadata keys leading to unbounded memory growth and OOM across all six nodes. Services degraded for 32 minutes as traffic fell through to database. Resolved by rolling restart with memory limits and TTL hotfix deployment.
